storyInfo: Maylander blows up the levitation layer, while people escape toward the border with the remaining ground structure. Nasti and her foster mother resolve their differences and say goodbye, and the tree's last missing piece is filled. When the smoke clears, the tree is the only thing left in the sky.

attributes.content: No sudden moves.
attributes.name: Agent A

attributes.content: You know, I'm sure we can talk about this like reasonable people—
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: All the rioters are here.
attributes.name: Agent A

attributes.content: Shut them in the lower level jail. We'll hand them to the courts when we arrive in D.C.
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: It's almost time. Double-check your parachutes unless you want to end up in a puddle.
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: What? Did someone press the detonate button early?
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: Impossible! We're less than a kilometer from the District... Wait, the government buildings are getting smaller... we're moving away from the capital?
attributes.name: Agent A

attributes.content: Looks like events are moving faster than your plans can catch up, aren't they?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: You never intended to stop the explosion... you were just stalling for time for the plate to turn?
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: Did you think we had the means to go toe-to-toe with well-trained units?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Events moving faster than the plans can catch up...
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: Change of plans.
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: Lower level, evacuate by the planned routes. Upper level, protect the civilians around you and prepare to parachute.
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: Point Zero, detonate.
attributes.name: Agent B

attributes.content: What's going on? The broadcast has gone dark... are the screens outside getting a signal?
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: You're still thinking about the broadcast?! Didn't you hear the Emergency Management Council's evacuation warning?
attributes.name: Tester

attributes.content: A serious accident has occurred in the levitation layer. All floating facilities will fall from the sky in less than ten minutes! Hurry up and get yourself to the emergency shelters!
attributes.name: Tester

attributes.content: Fall from the sky...?
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: Oakley looks up to see hundreds of floating pods turn into fireballs in the sky, marring the clear skies with smoke and ash.
Without the gentle touch of sunlight, the cloud-like pods turn back into chunks of metal, crashing down on the lower level.
Look out.
*cough*... that was a close call. Thanks.
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: You're a tester? Which company? I don't remember you...
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: Now is not the time for introductions, Oakley.
attributes.name: Tester?

attributes.content: The streets are exposed. You risk getting hit by falling debris. You should go into an emergency shelter quickly.
attributes.name: Tester?

attributes.content: A-Alright...
attributes.name: Oakley

attributes.content: Falling.
Tumbling down.
Ferne's heart is beating so hard, she feels like it might escape her ribcage at any moment.
She tries her best to control her breathing. The straps on the sides of the helmet slap her face, inflicting just enough pain to fend off the fear of falling.
In an instant, the signal from the detonator travels through the connected conduits, igniting all the floating facilities at the same time. Ferne leaps from Platform 7 before it loses all power.
The falling pod streaks past Ferne like a black meteor.
The bright-colored paint is burned off the advertisements, and melted cables flail in the air, giving off a bitter smell of tar.
The whole levitation layer sways meekly in the air like a deflated balloon.
'Lucky Cystybeast', 'One-Boot', 'Beachbrella'...
Ferne watches these company logos fall together with her. Now that is a scene that rivals sunset in the Bleached Wasteland, she thinks.
Pull the parachute, Ferne!
attributes.name: Aspen

attributes.content: Oops, almost forgot.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: The updraft lifts the Feline's slender frame. The parachutes come together to form a massive cloud.
Did we do it?
attributes.name: Aspen

attributes.content: The people on the ground don't know what's happening. It must be a shock for them to see their investments and products turn to ash...
attributes.name: Aspen

attributes.content: They're safe, at least. The pieces of the levitation layer would have fallen on top of their heads if the plate didn't turn around.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Besides, they'll never find out that there were scheming and plotting to stop them from dreaming.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Where do we go next?
attributes.name: Aspen

attributes.content: You could certainly go back to Northville to see your wife.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Sure. What about after that?
attributes.name: Aspen

attributes.content: After that?
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: Let's talk about that when we get back to standing on Terra Firma and looking up at the sky.
attributes.name: Ferne

attributes.content: The light of burning fire illuminates the people's faces, but there is little regret in their hearts as they gaze upon Propeller Paradise, now beyond recognition.
The remains of Propeller Paradise are scattered across the barrenlands between the District and the plate, like gifts that have been returned.
Ferne closes her eyes in relaxation, as she comes back down to earth with paradise.
The elderly Banshee said her kin were like broken kites as they fell burning from the sky in that war from two hundred years ago.
Now, though, she is like a kite that has too many strings attached, the branches and roots tugging at her as she floats in mid-air.
A kite nothing could ever ruin, as she had wished.
The Roosting Tree... that was the name I gave it. I built huts beneath it, and made it the abode of every Banshee.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But all I felt was fear when Laqeramaline brought me to its roots.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It was like a great void that could not be illuminated by the moons, by candles, or by fireflies. It was still and silent, sucking away my memory a little bit at a time...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I could smell decay in the darkness. A decay that we could not reverse. Not even the Children of Soil and Stone could.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It was a constant reminder of how pathetic the Convallis was.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: At the end, Lady Theresa and Lord Theresis found a future for the Sarkaz through war and death. Meanwhile, Aefanyl continues to seek a new future for the Banshees.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But me and my valley... it is nothing more than a failed sanctuary that quietly awaits death.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: And there's nothing more I can do for it.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: So you ran away from the Roosting Tree, ran away from death.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Why here, though?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Everywhere outside the Convallis was the same. Grey and chaotic, impossible to navigate.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: There was only one path that I remembered. Those unfamiliar names that you said... Leithanien, Laterano, Columbia...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I followed the names, followed your scent, not knowing to what end the path lead, and what I would do there... I just didn't want the tree to turn me into an empty husk.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But you chose to go into another tree.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I was filled with sorrow when I saw that you planted another Roosting Tree here.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: When I felt the spread of decay... I thought you were going to repeat our mistakes.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Even if we couldn't save the Convallis's Roosting Tree... I wanted to do something for this one.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Even if all I could do was stay its decay and save you from sorrow for a while...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But when I saw it with my own eyes, I realized... there was nothing I could do for it... but it could save me.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: The Roosting Tree only takes memories, but this one shares.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It shares the story of the Convallis, and of your journey... it makes me feel that I'm alive.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I'm sorry, Nasti. I had fused with the tree before I stopped the path from collapsing. Once again... I deceived you.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: ...That's why you're so lucid.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: That's why the tree rejects my incantations. You rewrote its grammar and changed its operating logic.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It keeps you alive, and you keep it running, even though neither of you are what you used to be.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: You're like a program that relies on a bug to keep running...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Bug...? Program...?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Like a withered tree that relies on a parasitic vine to remain standing.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It's not so bad, is it?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: If this can keep it from withering, then I've finally done something useful, for you, for the Banshees, for Sarkaz.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I can bring it back to the Convallis... bring you back.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: That would be pointless.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Why... why is the tree not responding to me?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: That means it's working.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The outer casing of the device Nasti is carrying on her belt falls away with a crack. Paper tapes pour out, laying themselves out around her as though following an invisible blueprint, forming an elaborate and organized framework.
The tapes are filled with tiny holes that represent incantations, coming together to build Nasti's unique language and safeguard her unique rules, rejecting the anarchic spread of the branches.
I said I must understand, before I can put an end to this error.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Now, I do.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti raises her pen and writes down a function, an incantation.
(Speaks in Sarkaz) Reconstruct.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: No... the memories that the tree shared with me... they're disappearing...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Nasti... Nasti! What are you doing?!
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Wiping the preloaded data in the tree. All that it shared with you was only temporary data for experimentation.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I know that you don't want to forget. But what you truly want to remember and retain are not these hotchpotch data put together for experiments.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Even if it's so...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Please, don't make the last thing I see be the sight of you destroying your life's work...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: You've walked such a long road and suffered so much for it...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: To you, the Convallis was your life's work. But I've never had one.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I have a goal. This tree is the means to it.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: My judgment of it is not based on how much I committed to it, only whether it can achieve my goal.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: You hoped that fusing with it would keep it from withering. But not withering won't make it any more useful.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Just like the Roosting Tree of the Convallis.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I don't want to be cruel, but the Roosting Tree never truly consoled the dying. It is no more than a simple phoebe tree, bent under the weight of incantations.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It's native to the genus Phoebopsis in the family of Targangils-Simaroubaceae, and its wood is commonly used for buildings in southeast Terra. I had an Elf colleague verify its nature after I joined Rhine Lab.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It became the Roosting Tree of the Convallis because you saw it as a symbol and mourned its change.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It has withered because it has reached the end of its natural lifespan.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: That is... all...?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Even the colors that run through its branches is merely the result of exposure to incantations over the years.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I don't remember how it used to look anymore...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But... Nasti... if it's truly so...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Why does your tree look so much like the Roosting Tree?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Because it reminds me of you.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Just as you think of me.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Do you know why I planted this 'tree'?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I remember the day we parted.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: You were calling to me, but I didn't turn back, because some words can no longer be said once you see the other person's face. But I couldn't help but look at your face reflected in the water—
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I wanted to remember how you looked.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: New tears flow across the lines made by old ones, as though it was a fixture on your face.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I spent more than a decade with you in the Convallis. I never saw you without tear marks on your face.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Life in the valley was good. I would have chosen the Convallis over Kazdel every time, a hundred times over. But Væla...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Was there a single moment that you weren't in pain, over the course of your two hundred years in the Convallis?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: At first, I thought I could wipe away your tears and make you smile with simple methods.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: You spent a whole day wiping my face with mistapple pulp.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It was only later that I learned why you were in pain.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The source of your pain was the decision, the lie.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The kin who suffered because they defied their nature; the kindred who could not return to the myriad souls because there was no one to deliver them. They were the source of your pain.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: To solve your problem, I must solve the problems of the Banshees, the problems of the Sarkaz.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The answer lies outside the Convallis, outside Kazdel, in a place that I don't know.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: As I indulged in such fantasies...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I saw you and I sail in the sky.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The reflected sky...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: It was a calm, windless day, but suddenly the water rippled without warning, wiping away the eternal tear marks on your face. You almost looked like you were smiling.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: And so, I began to think about the sky whenever I thought about how to make you smile.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Perhaps...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The sky is where we can find our sanctuary, and abode for our souls.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: (Speaks in Sarkaz) The Sarkaz's promised land could also be in the sky.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: All the things you did, this tree in the sky...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: That's where it all began.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Access security camera image.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Falling... like a broken kite...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Looks like Maylander successfully carried out their plan. The entire capital will bear witness to the fall of Propeller Paradise.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But the tremors came from the ground plate. Did Gordon...?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Will we fall too?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Not sure. I've undone the fusion and reset the tree, restoring it to an operable state.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But the tree itself is not yet complete.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: At least... save yourself, Nasti...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Three years ago, Aefanyl added a condition to my hypothesis when he delivered the myriad souls. Two years ago, a friend split the sky and showed me the path to the future.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But I'm still one step away from the answer. What is to come will depend on whether we find it.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: We're in the sky, but... I don't see the answer.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Everything has fallen... they sky is empty.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Yes, the sky has always been empty.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It is we who must fill it.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: For you, Væla, I don't think the answer is in the sky.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It's in you.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I can hear the roars, the hatred, the cries that surround you.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The kin I delivered, or failed to deliver... Vampires, Nachzehrers, Djaj... and Banshees.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I've wiped all the preloaded temporary experiment data from the tree.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: It's your own frustration, rage and sorrow that binds you now, like every Banshee that you sent away the last two hundred years, like every Sarkaz that returned to the myriad souls since the beginning of time.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: That is why our people are made empty by painful oblivion. That is why the myriad souls are always roaring, always angry, always wailing.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: But if we leave behind residues of information in death, and the myriad souls are merely the collective of such residues...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Why shouldn't it be joy and song that remains?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Does our nature prevent it?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: To process residues of information, build a server. If coverage on the ground isn't enough, raise it into the sky.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Not just a sanctuary for the living; the 'tree' will become an abode for the departed as well. This time, however, we decide what we leave behind.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Perhaps it wasn't function that the 'tree' was missing. It was a command, a decision, a sincere wish.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Tell me the answer, Væla. What is it that you truly want to leave behind?
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: The tree... I will preserve that memory for you.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Oblivion will never claim what you want to leave behind.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I don't know.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: You don't know...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: At first, I just wanted to save our kin from pain, from anger, from sorrow.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: But I no longer know how that can be achieved. If neither the Convallis nor the Roosting Tree could accomplish it... is there any meaning in leaving anything behind?
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Væla...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti wanted to do something, say something, but no sound comes out from her lips.
She is normally not one to deal with hypotheticals, but she could not help but think: If she finished the tree and took her people to the sanctuary in the sky sooner...
Would her mother have had a comforting memory to cling to?
Hundreds of pods fall burning from the sky, like black meteors, like heavy raindrops, like broken kites.
Mother and daughter gaze at each other silently, as they hoped it would make this moment an eternity.
No...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: You were never one to stop walking, Nasti. Once you've decided on a path, you'll see it through to the end.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: Don't stop now. Don't let me take away the meaning of what you've accomplished. My life may have had no meaning, but there must be some meaning to all that you've done...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: If this tree is the new abode for the souls of the Sarkaz, then let me be its first denizen.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I gave you a home twenty years ago. Now, you've given me one.
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: When the children stand around the tree to listen to its voice... I want it to be your laughter that they hear, not your weeping.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: And I want to remember your smile when I think of you...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti, my child...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I've had good memories... memories that made me smile...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: I remember... it was...
attributes.name: Væla

attributes.content: She cannot finish the sentence.
Half a year has passed since she realized that she was beginning to forget, and fled her hut under the Roosting Tree, following Nasti's trail all the way to Columbia.
A journey far too long for a dying Banshee.
It was not the reunion that she had hoped for. But as the obsession that carried her to this point falls apart, death has finally caught up to her.
Countless scenes fade from Væla's memories, and become vivid before Nasti's eyes.

attributes.text: The Banshee's oar lies still in her hands.
\nShe hears a lament. The king who painted the same dream with her has passed, in a faraway place that she cannot see.
The Banshee stands still beneath the Roosting Tree.
\nShe spent three months witnessing her kin fall victim to oblivion and become an empty husk, the price they paid for her lies, and there was nothing she could do about it.
The Banshee kneels still on the floor of a dilapidated house.
\nShe promised to deliver a Gargoyle who helped her in the past, but all that was left was the tip of a petrified tail.
The Banshee's sorrow and regrets are infinite, flowing past Nasti like a river without end.
\nAfter all of these memories have faded, there is one last thing that is left. One tiny, brief fragment.
The Banshee is putting a child to bed, a girl whom she brought back to the Convallis.
\nHer eyes are filled with fatigue as she looks at her old friend, but all she would talk about is how lovely the child is.
She used to be a child, once.
\nShe opens her eyes and sees this land for the first time. She does not know whether it is good or bad.
But Nasti can see that she is smiling.

attributes.content: Time passes through the Banshee, like the wind across the surface of Mist Lake, leaving nothing behind.
Væla fades away into the sky.
A leaf gently drifts down.
I'll catch you, and remember you...
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Mother.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: Nasti catches the leaf gently, as her mother once picked a leaf from the tree for her.
...One does not need a bone whistle to accompany a smile.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: After a long silence, she blows a tune with the leaf.
Like threads, the notes weave the gentlest incantation that she has ever weaved.
Be at peace.
attributes.name: Nasti

attributes.content: I'd like to make a bet with you, Miss Wilson.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: How many sky stocks do you think young Pavard, that honest and serious secretary of mine, will go and buy once he delivers the balloons?
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Zero. The end of the line at the stock exchange was two blocks away when I came.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: The last time I saw this level of excitement in the District was when the Department of Defense decided to send troops to Bolívar. I still have one of those cactus hats in my office.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: People went into the streets wearing those hats, shouting slogans and turning the veins of the D.C. green.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I haven't seen those hats in a long time.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: No one wants their taxes turn into guns, not since Robert McKesson and his feat of 'saving Bolívar from the Bolívarians' became the butt of evening radio's jokes.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Everybody wears a propeller on their heads now.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: A blue propeller hat that spins. It's awfully noisy, to tell the truth.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I hear there's a new version that automatically plays that rock song every Columbian knows when the propeller spins.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I grew up listening to 'Terra Bless Us' and 'Cityway 66'. I once thought that the song of our time would be 'Goodnight, Arc-01.'
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Turns out it would be 'Good Night, Kristen Wright.'
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: The obsession that Columbians have with the future will never change, but the one they're following seems to have changed while we weren't looking.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Kristen never intended to become an idol.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: The Department of Defense never intended to become a joke.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: It never occurred to any of us, when we raised a glass to the failure of the DOD, that it wouldn't be just the DOD who loses credibility.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: The people listening to late-night radio over a pot of mac & cheese don't differentiate between the DOD, Congress, or the cabinet, after all.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: As you said yourself, these days, the masses seem more willing to take inspiration from a legend or miracle.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Sometimes, however, we need to turn their gazes back to the soil that nurtures miracle.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Back to Columbia.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Finally, we lay our eyes on Propeller Paradise. Not a day has passed that we don't think about it, ever since that fateful night two years ago...
attributes.name: Tech Review Reporter

attributes.content: The synchronized aerial turn that we just witnessed is a testament to the skill of our test pilots, even in times of peace.
attributes.name: The Savior Special Guest

attributes.content: Now, Propeller Paradise reveals its miracles to use, this wonderful spectacle of the Columbian Dream—
attributes.name: Voice of Progress Official Channel

attributes.content: Demonstrating that—
attributes.name: Tech Review Reporter

attributes.content: Declaring that—
attributes.name: The Savior Special Guest

attributes.content: Silence.
Every media outlet, every channel, voices rich or coarse or sharp or warm, all fall silent at the same time.
The entrepreneurs and investors on the streets, along with thousands upon thousands of ordinary people, fall silent too.
For a few minutes, the only sound in Max, D.C. is the sound of their propeller hats.
The rustling sound they make, like falling leaves, is the only accompaniment to this spectacular disaster, witnessed by millions in the capital.
...'turn their gazes back to the soil that nurtures miracle.'
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Back to Columbia.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: A terrorist attack?
attributes.name: Voice of Progress Official Channel

attributes.content: ...A premeditated act of terror.
attributes.name: Tech Review Reporter

attributes.content: Investigators confirm that the explosion was caused by a batch of Arts weapons smuggled in disguised as general parts.
attributes.name: The Savior Special Guest

attributes.content: The weapons can be traced to numerous centrum nations, including...
attributes.name: The Savior Special Guest

attributes.content: Kazimierz, which has long since abandoned the ways of chivalry, and Leithanien, the ancient nation of casters, have used their despicable tricks against us.
attributes.name: Tech Review Reporter

attributes.content: The voice of progress falls silent today. It is the darkest day in Columbia's eighty years of history...
attributes.name: Voice of Progress Official Channel

attributes.content: The nation is waiting for me to speak.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I should tell them: 'They have destroyed more than just an exhibition; they have destroyed the hopes and dreams that every Columbian entrusted to it.'
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I should tell them: 'This shameless, heinous act was committed by those who envy our splendor, trying to make our people timid and afraid.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I should tell them: 'But they're doomed to fail. Columbia will never be defeated. No conspiracy can stop the Lamp and Star from shining bright in the west.'
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: And then, Columbians will eagerly turn their taxes into guns again?
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Everything that they fervently devoted to Propeller Paradise, all those sky stocks, and the spinning propeller hats, will turn into shields and swords.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: When the smoke clears, the empty sky will be more inflammatory than any speech.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: The winds on the wilderness of Columbia have never stopped, mercilessly blowing away the smoke and cloud that surrounded Propeller Paradise.
The sky feels even emptier than before, after all things bright and beautiful have fallen.
But it's not entirely empty.
Is the soot playing tricks on my eyes?
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I see it as well as you do, Mr. Vice President.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: As do millions of people here in D.C.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Floating platforms supported by a complicated system of massive hot-air balloons and propellers have fallen. Floating labs of many shapes and sizes have fallen.

The ropeway system that connected the levitation layer has fallen, along with hundreds of pods. The saucer-shaped tower that linked the ground and the sky has fallen, along with the elevator that it supports.
Only one thing rises through the clouds, into the bright skies of Columbia.
Message from Maylander, sir.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: Propeller Paradise has stopped moving toward the capital. It is now headed for the southeastern border, accelerating.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: Maylander believes they are trying to escape Columbia, which would be an act of treason.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: Mechanized units on the border are moving to cut off Propeller Paradise's potential routes.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: More importantly, the unknown object in the sky has already been locked on by our defensive artillery deployed along the District's route.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: With your speech about to begin, they want to hear how you define it.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: The Department of Defense recommends opening fire immediately, and sticking with the current narrative. Maylander has raised the possibility of conveying a different message through the survival of this facility.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: But they have also noted the risks of doing so. The facility was created by Rhine Lab, after all.
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: Before they do anything, I would like to get a good look at this thing myself.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: A tree?
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: A tree.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Rhine certainly has style, doesn't it? A tree-shaped facility. Do you know what it is?
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I have no idea. I suppose I'm just here to lighten the mood while you vent about Rhine.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Heh. Well, it doesn't matter.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: What it is will soon stop mattering to the people, just like that balloon that couldn't fly.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: In the DOD's game, it will become an infant slaughtered by knights, an envoy killed by the Witch King. Its fall will drive the rise of the people's wrath.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: In Maylander's proposal, it will become the next symbol of the Columbian spirit, its silhouette printed on every hat that leaves the factories for the next decade.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Is that so? All I see is a tree.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: You see those fowlbeasts, brushing their feathers and honing their beaks?
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: They've accepted that it's a tree. A tree that they can roost on.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: A tree rising from the wounds of Columbia? How should people understand it?
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: They don't have to.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Just as you didn't need to understand the mobile mining platform on the wilderness that you skipped classes to watch when you were young.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: Did you know if it was carrying a group of ambitious pioneers, or desperate debtors? Did it return with a full load of riches, or was it simply drifting aimlessly?
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: ...Hah.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: All I know is that I really didn't want to take a test that day, and the mining platform happened to be chasing the setting sun.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: A mining platform chasing the setting sun, and a flying tree.
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: They're not so different, are they?
attributes.name: Jara

attributes.content: What is your position, Mr. Vice President?
attributes.name: Vice President's Secretary

attributes.content: I'm the vice president of the Columbian Union. With this position, comes responsibility.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I can't skip classes to watch a mining platform chase the setting sun, like I used to.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: Still...
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: That was, indeed, what made me feel something about the soil of Columbia for the first time.
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: If the sky is the soil that nurtures the future of Columbia...
attributes.name: Jackson

attributes.content: I'm sure there's enough room in it for a flying tree.
attributes.name: Jackson
